hypothesis: Incorporating explicit solvent molecule clustering patterns observed in molecular dynamics simulations as additional graph augmentation features into LoQI's architecture will improve conformer generation accuracy for highly polar or charged molecules by more than 20% in median relative energy compared to the current CPCM-only implicit solvent training approach, because the discrete first-solvation-shell geometry encodes directional hydrogen-bonding constraints that implicit continuum models systematically fail to capture and that the current graph augmentation scheme does not represent.
